reasoning: The FRED combined statistical model estimates a 0.56 probability of an increase in the short term, driven heavily by positive seasonality (the index historically rises from mid-August into September). In addition to the positive seasonality, the general series trend over the last 3-4 months has been strongly positive, rebounding from an intra-year low of 61.35 in May 2025 up to 66.19 in August 2025, which reflects a broader, albeit recent, stabilization and recovery in tech hiring. However, the last recorded value of 66.19 on the forecast date represents a slight near-term "spike" relative to the prior week's average (around 66.05), warranting slight caution against short-term mean reversion. Overall, the evidence firmly leans towards an ongoing positive trajectory in the immediate weeks, justifying an initial forecast just above the FRED estimate (at 0.57) to reflect multi-month upward momentum. Following strictly the instruction to increase uncertainty monotonically with forecast horizon length, the estimates fade closer to 0.5 for the more distant dates, reflecting the inherent unpredictability of the longer-term tech job market across 6 months.
